system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     pimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          0.0025;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(p)         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|k|omega|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    "(U|k|omega|epsilon)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    2;
    nCorrectors         1;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;

laminar
{
    laminarModel    generalizedNewtonian;

    generalizedNewtonianCoeffs
    {
        viscosityModel  CrossPowerLaw;

        nuInf   10;
        m       0.4;
        n       3;

        tauStar   6.25;
    }

    nu              0.01;
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

viscosityModel  constant;

nu              0.01;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - bottom left (below cylinder)
    (-1 -0.6 -0.5)    // 0
    (-0.4 -0.6 -0.5)  // 1
    (-0.4 -0.2 -0.5)  // 2
    (-1 -0.2 -0.5)    // 3

    (-1  0.6 -0.5)    // 4
    (-0.4  0.6 -0.5)  // 5
    (-0.4  0.2 -0.5)  // 6
    (-1  0.2 -0.5)    // 7

    // Block 1 - bottom right (below cylinder)
    (-0.4 -0.6 -0.5)  // 8
    ( 0.4 -0.6 -0.5)  // 9
    ( 0.4 -0.2 -0.5)  // 10
    (-0.4 -0.2 -0.5)  // 11

    (-0.4  0.6 -0.5)  // 12
    ( 0.4  0.6 -0.5)  // 13
    ( 0.4  0.2 -0.5)  // 14
    (-0.4  0.2 -0.5)  // 15

    // Block 2 - top left (above cylinder)
    (-1  0.6 -0.5)    // 16
    (-0.4  0.6 -0.5)  // 17
    (-0.4  1.4 -0.5)  // 18
    (-1  1.4 -0.5)    // 19

    // Block 3 - top right (above cylinder)
    (-0.4  0.6 -0.5)  // 20
    ( 0.4  0.6 -0.5)  // 21
    ( 0.4  1.4 -0.5)  // 22
    (-0.4  1.4 -0.5)  // 23

    // Block 4 - far right
    ( 0.4 -0.6 -0.5)  // 24
    ( 2.4 -0.6 -0.5)  // 25
    ( 2.4 -0.2 -0.5)  // 26
    ( 0.4 -0.2 -0.5)  // 27

    ( 0.4  0.6 -0.5)  // 28
    ( 2.4  0.6 -0.5)  // 29
    ( 2.4  0.2 -0.5)  // 30
    ( 0.4  0.2 -0.5)  // 31

    // Block 5 - far left
    (-2.4 -0.6 -0.5)  // 32
    (-1.4 -0.6 -0.5)  // 33
    (-1.4 -0.2 -0.5)  // 34
    (-2.4 -0.2 -0.5)  // 35

    (-2.4  0.6 -0.5)  // 36
    (-1.4  0.6 -0.5)  // 37
    (-1.4  0.2 -0.5)  // 38
    (-2.4  0.2 -0.5)  // 39
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)        (20 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15)  (20 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    hex (16 17 18 19 17 18 19 16) (20 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    hex (20 21 22 23 21 22 23 20) (20 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    hex (24 25 26 27 28 29 30 31) (60 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    hex (32 33 34 35 36 37 38 39) (60 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

// Edges sub-dictionary removed to avoid arc collinearity issues

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
            (32 36 39 35)
            (33 37 38 34)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (25 26 27 24)
            (29 30 31 28)
        );
    }
    top
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (19 18 17 16)
            (23 22 21 20)
            (39 38 37 36)
            (35 34 33 32)
        );
    }
    bottom
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 2 3)
            (8 9 10 11)
            (24 25 26 27)
            (32 33 34 35)
        );
    }
    cylinder
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (17 16 15 14 11 10 9 8)
            (18 19 20 21 15 14 13 12)
        );
    }
    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            // front faces (z = -0.5)
            (0 1 5 4)
            (8 9 13 12)
            (16 17 21 20)
            (20 21 25 24)
            (24 25 29 28)
            (32 33 37 36)
            (36 37 38 39)

            // back faces (z = 0.5)
            (3 2 6 7)
            (11 10 14 15)
            (23 22 26 27)
            (27 26 30 31)
            (28 29 31 30)
            (35 34 38 39)
            (39 38 37 36)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volVectorField;
    object      U;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform (0 0 0);

boundaryField

    }

    {

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Block Has Inward-Pointing Faces

**Error Message:**
```
Block hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) ... has inward-pointing faces
    4(0 3 2 1)
```

**Diagnosis:**

The fundamental geometric problem is that the vertex ordering in several hex blocks produces degenerate/inverted cells. Looking at the vertex coordinates:

- Block 0: vertices 0=(-1,-0.6), 1=(-0.4,-0.6), 2=(-0.4,-0.2), 3=(-1,-0.2), 4=(-1,0.6), 5=(-0.4,0.6), 6=(-0.4,0.2), 7=(-1,0.2)

  This block spans from x=-1 to x=-0.4 and y=-0.6 to y=0.6. The issue is that vertices 0→1→2→3 go from left-bottom to right-bottom to right-top to left-top, which means the block is oriented such that the normal of face (0 3 2 1) points **into** the block rather than outward. The vertex ordering needs to follow the right-hand rule consistently.

- Similar issues affect Blocks 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5 due to inconsistent vertex ordering throughout the `blockMeshDict`.

**Resolution:**

Redefine the vertex ordering in all hex blocks to ensure consistent right-handed orientation. For a hex block with vertices indexed as:

```
0-----1
|    /|
|   / |
|  /  |
| /   |
3-----2
|    /|
|   / |
|  /  |
| /   |
7-----6
```

The standard convention is that face (0 1 2 3) is the bottom face and face (4 5 6 7) is the top face, with z-axis coming out of the page. Apply this consistently:

- **Block 0**: Change from `(0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)` to `(0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5)`
- **Block 1**: Change from `(8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15)` to `(8 11 10 9 12 15 14 13)`
- **Block 2**: Change from `(16 17 18 19 17 18 19 16)` to `(16 19 18 17 17 19 18 16)` — wait, this block references duplicate vertices. Rewrite entirely as `(16 19 18 17 17 19 18 16)` becomes problematic. Better: `(16 19 18 17 17 19 18 16)` isn't valid. Redefine Block 2 as `(16 17 18 19 17 18 19 16)` → change to `(16 19 18 17 17 19 18 16)`... No, the cleanest fix: rewrite Block 2 as `(16 19 18 17 17 19 18 16)` — actually, redefine it as `(16 17 18 19 17 18 19 16)` → `(16 19 18 17 17 19 18 16)` still wrong. Let me reconsider the vertex layout.**

Actually, re-examining the vertex definitions, the better approach is to restructure the entire mesh to avoid these degeneracies. The current vertex layout has many duplicated vertices and incorrect block connectivity. Here's a cleaner approach:

**Proposed fix for `blockMeshDict`:**

1. Remove the `arc` edges sub-dictionary entirely (it's commented out anyway).
2. Restructure the vertex numbering and block definitions to create a simpler, non-degenerate mesh. A practical approach:

   - Define only the essential vertices around the cylinder and the domain boundaries.
   - Create blocks that don't share inverted orientations.
   - One recommended approach: simplify to 3 blocks (bottom-left below cylinder, bottom-right below cylinder, top above cylinder) with the cylinder hole carved out through shared faces.

However, given the complexity, the most reliable fix is:

**Remove Blocks 2, 3, 4, 5 entirely** and redefine just two blocks (the bottom row below the cylinder and the top row above the cylinder) with proper vertex ordering, OR more practically:

**Rewrite the entire `blocks` section** with corrected vertex orderings. For example:

```
blocks
(
    hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5)        (20 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    hex (8 11 10 9 12 15 14 13)  (20 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    hex (16 19 18 17 17 19 18 16) (20 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    hex (20 23 22 21 21 23 22 20) (20 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    hex (24 27 26 25 28 31 30 29) (60 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    hex (32 35 34 33 36 39 38 37) (60 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);
```

But even better — since the previous attempt showed arc-related problems, **eliminate all arc definitions** and use only straight edges. The root cause across all attempts is that the vertex layout combined with arc specifications keeps creating degenerate geometries. Without knowing the exact desired cylinder shape, the safest recommendation is:

**Replace the entire `blocks` section with a simplified, validated configuration.** Based on the offsetCylinder tutorial pattern, define vertices forming a rectangular domain with a cylindrical obstacle, ensuring all hex blocks have consistent right-hand-oriented vertex ordering. For example, a minimal working version could have fewer blocks with properly ordered vertices.

## Error 2: `log.pimpleFoam` — Cannot Find "points"

**Diagnosis:**

This is purely a cascading failure. Since `blockMesh` failed, no mesh exists, so `pimpleFoam` cannot find the mesh files.

**Resolution:** Fix `blockMeshDict` first, then re-run `blockMesh` followed by `pimpleFoam`.

## Summary of Required Changes

1. **`system/blockMeshDict`**: Completely rewrite the `blocks` section with correct, non-inverted hex vertex orderings. Keep the `arc` edges section removed (use straight edges only). Ensure all boundary face definitions reference the correct vertex indices after restructuring.
2. **`0/p`**: The `defaultFaces` patch named `empty` may conflict with the actual patch name used in `blockMeshDict`. Verify that once the mesh is generated, the patch name matches exactly. Consider removing `defaultFaces` and listing all six patches explicitly (`inlet`, `outlet`, `top`, `bottom`, `cylinder`, `frontAndBack`), with `frontAndBack` set to `type empty;` in both `0/U` and `0/p`.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Replace entire blocks section with corrected vertex orderings: hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5) (20 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1); hex (8 11 10 9 12 15 14 13) (20 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1); hex (16 19 18 17 17 19 18 16) (20 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1); hex (20 23 22 21 21 23 22 20) (20 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1); hex (24 27 26 25 28 31 30 29) (60 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1); hex (32 35 34 33 36 39 38 37) (60 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1); Replace inlet boundary faces with (0 4 7 3) and (32 36 39 35) and (33 37 38 34); Replace outlet boundary faces with (25 26 27 24) and (29 30 31 28); Replace top boundary faces with (19 18 17 16) and (23 22 21 20) and (39 38 37 36) and (35 34 33 32); Replace bottom boundary faces with (0 1 2 3) and (8 9 10 11) and (24 25 26 27) and (32 33 34 35); Replace cylinder boundary faces with (17 16 15 14 11 10 9 8) and (18 19 20 21 15 14 13 12); Replace frontAndBack boundary faces with front: (0 1 5 4) and (8 9 13 12) and (16 17 21 20) and (20 21 25 24) and (24 25 29 28) and (32 33 37 36) and (36 37 38 39); Replace back faces with (3 2 6 7) and (11 10 14 15) and (23 22 26 27) and (27 26 30 31) and (28 29 31 30) and (35 34 38 39) and (39 38 37 36)'}, {'file': '0/p', 'changes': 'Rename defaultFaces patch to frontAndBack to match the blockMeshDict patch name; keep type empty'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a laminar offsetcylinder simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm with a generalized Newtonian model and Cross-Power-Law viscosity for momentum transport, characterized by specific parameters nuInf=10, m=0.4, n=3. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 1m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls, including the cylinder surface. Use timestep of 0.0025 and output every 0.05. Finaltime is 0.5. use constant viscosity model with nu value of 0.01 in physical properties.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
